questions.question_text: In New Mexico, residential property is assessed for tax purposes at what fraction of market value?
questions.explanation: NM assesses both residential and non-residential property at one-third (33.33%) of market value. The county assessor determines market value, then calculates the assessed value.

questions.question_text: A NM home has a market value of $300,000. What is the assessed value for tax purposes?
questions.explanation: NM assesses property at 1/3 of market value. $300,000 × 1/3 = $100,000 assessed value. Tax rates are then applied to the assessed value, not the full market value.

questions.question_text: Which official determines the market value of property for NM tax purposes?
questions.explanation: The county assessor determines property values for tax purposes. The county treasurer collects the taxes. These are separate roles in NM's property tax system.

questions.question_text: In NM, property tax liens generally take priority over:
questions.correct_answer: Most other liens on the property
distractors: No other liens on the property
questions.explanation: Property tax liens take priority over most other liens, including mortgages and mechanic's liens. Only certain federal liens may take precedence over property tax liens.

questions.question_text: Who is responsible for collecting property taxes in NM?
questions.explanation: The county treasurer collects property taxes in NM. The county assessor values the property — these are two distinct functions in the property tax system.

questions.question_text: The NM head of family property tax exemption reduces the assessed value by:
questions.explanation: The head of family exemption reduces the assessed value (not market value) by $2,000 for qualifying heads of household in New Mexico.

questions.question_text: A disabled veteran in NM qualifies for a property tax exemption of:
questions.explanation: Disabled veterans in NM receive a $4,000 reduction in assessed value. This is in addition to any other applicable exemptions like the head of family exemption.

questions.question_text: Property taxes in NM become delinquent, and the owner does not pay for several years. A tax sale of the property can occur after:
questions.explanation: Delinquent property taxes in NM can result in a tax sale after 3 years of non-payment. This gives owners a reasonable period to cure the delinquency before losing the property.

questions.question_text: NM property taxes are due in how many installments per year?
questions.explanation: NM property taxes are due in two installments: the first half by November 10 and the second half by April 10 of the following year.

questions.question_text: The first installment of NM property taxes is due by:
questions.explanation: The first half of NM property taxes is due by November 10. The second half is due by April 10. The tax year runs from January 1 to December 31.

questions.question_text: A general contractor who is not paid for work on a NM property must file a mechanic's lien within:
questions.explanation: In NM, an original (general) contractor must file a mechanic's lien within 120 days of project completion. Subcontractors and suppliers have a shorter 90-day deadline.

questions.question_text: A subcontractor wants to preserve their lien rights on a NM project. They must first serve:
questions.correct_answer: A preliminary notice to the owner
distractors: A cease and desist to the general contractor
A complaint with the state licensing board
questions.explanation: Subcontractors and material suppliers must serve a preliminary notice on the property owner to preserve their mechanic's lien rights in New Mexico.

questions.question_text: A mechanic's lien in NM relates back to which date?
questions.correct_answer: The date work commenced on the project
distractors: The date the lien was filed
The date the contract was signed
The date payment was first due
questions.explanation: NM mechanic's liens relate back to the date work commenced on the project. This means the lien's priority is determined by when work began, not when the lien was filed.

questions.question_text: A property owner pays a contractor in full and wants proof that no lien will be filed. The owner can require the contractor to provide:
questions.explanation: Property owners can require a lien waiver from contractors upon payment. This document releases the contractor's right to file a mechanic's lien for the work covered by the payment.

questions.question_text: A mechanic's lien attaches to which type of property in NM?
distractors: Only the improvements, not the land
The owner's other real estate holdings
questions.explanation: Mechanic's liens attach to both the real property (land) and the improvements. The lien encumbers the property where the work was performed.
